If there be an ant-hill to the north of a knotty 6ami tree (Saen)
there will be water at a depth of 250 cubits at a distance of 5 cubits
to its west.

When there are five ant-hills in one and the same place and
the central one white, there is said to be a water-vein below the
central one at a depth of 275 cubits.

Where there is a Sami tree joined with a pala^a (Buteo fron-
dosa) tree, there will be water 300 cubits below at a distance of
5 cubits to its west. Where the earth is dug 2^ cubits, there will
be a snake at first, and then yellow clay mixed with gravel.

Where there is a white Rohltaka tree surrounded by an ant-
hill, there will be water at a depth of 350 cubits and one cubit to
the east.
